entries.golden_scenario: A worktree-isolated builder ends its run with uncommitted work (happened 4x in the 2026-07-13/14 session: background-and-end, process restarts, mid-fix reboot; recovery each time depended on the orchestrator noticing — memory-rung). Gate blocks the stop ONCE with exact preserve commands (commit / stash -u / waiver).
entries.fp_expectation: OBSERVE-FIRST (blocking:false): logs would-block verdicts, blocks nothing. FP model corrected per harness-review 2026-07-17: read-only reviewers are silent ONLY because each dispatched agent gets its OWN fresh worktree (clean at stop) — the gate has no role signal; a read-only agent that leaves scratch files in its worktree IS a would-block, and the enforce-mode message names stash as the right choice for throwaway dirt. Structural exclusions: non-pool cwd silent (S3); no pwd fallback (S5); one block per stop via stop_hook_active (S4) PLUS a 10-min per-session marker bounding the loop even if the platform never sets the flag (S9).
entries.retirement_condition: Retire if the platform gains first-class commit-on-stop enforcement for worktree subagents, or if worktree isolation is retired. Re-evaluate if the probe log (~/.claude/state/agent-commit-gate-probe.jsonl) shows SubagentStop events carry no usable cwd (gate would be permanently silent = theater) — then rehost the check.
entries.honesty_rationale: OBSERVE-FIRST ROLLOUT (harness-review 2026-07-17 REFORMULATE, block-mode-before-FP-calibrated): default computes + logs the verdict (probe records raw cwd, session_id, stop_active, would_block, outcome; rotated ~200KB) and exits 0. FLIP CRITERION to AGENT_COMMIT_GATE_ENFORCE=1 + blocking:true — ALL THREE on real probe data: (1) cwd identifies the STOPPING agent's OWN agent-<id> worktree, never a parent/orchestrator tree (the reviewed data-loss tail: a false-blocked subagent stashing the orchestrator's LIVE WIP); (2) stop_hook_active observed set on a blocked retry (correlated by session_id); (3) zero false would-blocks over the observation window. Covers CLEAN stops only — crashes/SIGKILL/reboot fire no hook (detection layer: orphaned-worktree-guard, held separately). Post-stop writes by backgrounded children are invisible to a stop-time check.

entries.golden_scenario: Pre-architecture-reviewer era: every reviewer this harness had tested correctness-against-spec, and "not one attacked the SHAPE of the design" (doctrine/artifact-evidence-bar-full.md). A candidate agent Written today with polished, reasonable-sounding prose but no named failure modes, no canon, no GOLDEN CASE — the exact shape those pre-bar agents had — is GATE 2's target: self-test S4 constructs exactly this file (a 'shape-reviewer' whose entire body is 'read the proposal, think about whether it seems reasonable, approve it') and the gate blocks the Write (rc=2).
entries.fp_expectation: Zero retroactive false positives by construction: the gate distinguishes new-vs-existing by ON-DISK PRESENCE at PreToolUse time (grandfather clause), so all ~25 pre-bar agents are unconditionally allowed on every Edit/MultiEdit/overwrite (self-test S3, S6). For genuinely new agents, six of the seven property checks are broad OR-lists of vocabulary already standard across this harness's own agent corpus (verified against architecture-reviewer.md, the reference agent this bar was built to codify — it independently clears all six); the one hard requirement, a literal '## GOLDEN CASE' heading with >= 30 non-whitespace chars, is unambiguous and fully in the author's control (self-test S7 proves a placeholder-thin GOLDEN CASE section still blocks). Residual false-positive class: a new agent with a real golden case that never uses the heading text 'GOLDEN CASE' verbatim (e.g. 'Reference Incident' instead) would be wrongly blocked — accepted as a known, narrow gap rather than loosening the one unambiguous check to fuzzy heading-matching.
entries.retirement_condition: Retire the on-disk grandfather heuristic if every existing agent is eventually brought up to the full bar (at that point the distinction collapses and the check can apply uniformly to Edit too). Retire the gate entirely if agent authorship moves to a workflow where architecture-reviewer/harness-reviewer review every new agent file BEFORE it is ever Written (making the point-of-Write gate redundant with an earlier review-time control).
entries.honesty_rationale: Blocking, but the only failure mode is 'add a GOLDEN CASE section and cite the seven properties' — always resolvable by the author in the same turn, no external dependency; grandfathered agents can never be blocked so there is no retroactive-breakage risk. OBSERVE-FIRST ROLLOUT (harness-review 2026-07-17, same discipline as agent-commit-gate): emit_block records the would-block verdict to ~/.claude/state/agent-design-gate-probe.jsonl and ALLOWS unless AGENT_DESIGN_GATE_ENFORCE=1. Flip to enforce (+blocking:true, budget re-check) after N real fires with zero false positives in the probe log. Resolves the D5 blocking budget to within 13 (was 14/13 RED).

entries.honest_status: scripts/agent-heartbeat.sh (emit/conclude/watch/reap) — per-AGENT liveness heartbeat in the heartbeats/agents/ namespace (2026-07-14 lesson: background agents need a push heartbeat + watchdog, not orchestrator output-polling). watch AND reap are spliced into hooks/stalled-work-surfacer.sh run() (watch surfaces stalled agents at SessionStart alongside stalled Workflow runs; reap bounds the namespace — the session-heartbeat reaper's 2026-07-09 dead-reaper defect, avoided here by wiring reap in the same commit). conclude is the terminal beat: an agent self-removes on clean completion so a COMPLETED agent is never surfaced as stalled (mirrors the workflow half's started==result suppression; without it every finished agent false-fires — the cry-wolf that REFORMULATE'd orphaned-worktree-guard, fixed here per harness-review CONDITIONAL-PASS 2026-07-14). INTERIM PATTERN: relies on the dispatched agent calling emit/conclude (dispatch-prompt convention in doctrine/background-work-tracking.md); the true runtime auto-heartbeat is not in-repo. Detection covers agents that emitted then stopped (the worked-then-wedged class). Not event-wired as its own settings.json entry — a splice inside the already-wired stalled-work-surfacer.sh; inventory-only per the manifest-check disk-coverage note (scripts/ is not disk-scanned).

entries.waiver_path: teammate-spawn-validator.sh condition (d) honors a DAG-review structured waiver via lib/waiver-purpose-clause.sh. NOTE: task-created-validator.sh and task-completed-evidence-gate.sh (same entry, different hook files) have GAP-class bypasses (env-var, not ledger-logged/fresh) — tracked as orchestratorTodo, not yet fixed.

entries.honest_status: PATTERN, backed by two real MECHANISMS as of 2026-07-14 (this is the doctrine entry; the two gates are their own manifest entries with their own golden_scenario/fp_expectation/retirement_condition). (1) GATE 1 = plan-reviewer.sh Check 17 (see the 'plan-reviewer' entry): blocking, fires on Status: ACTIVE (or unset) plans whose text matches a tight architecture-keyword set (source of truth / read-write path / staleness / materialize / derived store / cache-invalidation / sync engine-job / projection / reconcile loop — calibrated against this repo's own 237-plan corpus to 27.0% hit rate after a bare-stem draft measured 50.6%), requiring a linked docs/reviews/*-architecture-review.md whose verdict is SOUND or SOUND-WITH-AMENDMENTS (NEEDS-RESHAPING does not satisfy it); 7/7 self-test scenarios including the real cockpit-v2-push-materialized-store.md/2026-07-14-cockpit-v2-architecture-review.md NEEDS-RESHAPING golden case. (2) GATE 2 = the 'agent-design-gate' entry (adapters/claude-code/hooks/agent-design-gate.sh): PreToolUse on Edit|Write|MultiEdit, blocks a brand-new adapters/claude-code/agents/*.md Write lacking a '## GOLDEN CASE' section + evidence of the seven properties; grandfathers all pre-bar agents by on-disk-existence check; 7/7 self-test scenarios. Operator directive 2026-07-14.

scripts/ask-registry.sh — the ask-registry CLI (register/attach-session/link-plan/set-status/merge/override-project, plus the cockpit-roadmap-redesign Task 2 work-item-layer verbs: set-title/capture-candidate/classify-candidate/detach-candidate/amend) writing ~/.claude/state/ask-registry.jsonl plus a best-effort in-repo mirror at docs/asks/ask-registry.jsonl (path resolved via nl_main_checkout_root, never a worktree) and a heuristic-first summarizer (optional ASK_SUMMARIZER=haiku upgrade, async, best-effort; the SAME gated async lane classifies amendment candidates amendment/noise — best-effort, candidates stay honestly pending on any failure). Title records carry title_source auto|operator; readers MUST fold operator-beats-auto regardless of timestamp (the A3 precedence contract in the script header). Called by hooks/workstreams-read.sh's capture splices (register on the first operator prompt; capture-candidate on every later prompt of an ask-attached session — both guarded by hooks/lib/progress-log-lib.sh's pl_classify_session against spawned/builder sessions) and by hooks/session-start-digest.sh's session-attach splice (attach-session, beside the existing heartbeat splice); set-status/merge are also called by the workstreams-ui server's POST /api/ask/<id>/lifecycle endpoint (operator override) and by the background auditor (mechanical completion). Not event-wired as its own settings.json entry — a one-line splice call-site inside already-wired hooks, mirroring the session-heartbeat/ensure-cockpit convention. Inventory-only per the filed nl-issue note that manifest-check's disk-coverage check (b) only disk-scans hooks/*.sh top-level, never scripts/ — this entry exists for honesty, not enforcement.
Dispatched via session-start-surfacer-pack.sh since D.5 (one SessionStart entry); E.1 digest replaces the pack.
invoked via pre-commit-gate.sh chain; not directly wired in settings.json.template
entries.honesty_rationale: Commit-time atomicity check (new plan must declare absorbed backlog items in its own header) — resolvable by editing the same commit; not a world-state assertion.

entries.honest_status: Invoked by stop-verdict-dispatcher.sh in --report mode (E.11, §E.W); no longer a direct Stop-chain entry. --self-test + blocking logic intact.
entries.waiver_path: ATTEST_FILE (structured waiver via lib/waiver-purpose-clause.sh, waiver_has_purpose_clauses gate) — the canonical reference implementation other hooks were retrofitted to match.
CLAUDE_MD_HYGIENE_DISABLE=1 env var (full no-op); note DEFAULT MODE is warn (exit 0 always) pending 24h calibration — blocking:true reflects the designed end-state.

entries.golden_scenario: Lesson 2026-07-11 (docs/lessons/2026-07-11-bulk-shared-state-mutation-without-ownership-check.md, nl-issue [35]): a session under operator pressure bulk-flips every ACTIVE docs/plans/*.md to DEFERRED and pushes to master, including a plan whose feature branch is checked out RIGHT THEN in another same-machine worktree — the gate blocks the Status flip / git mv and names the owning worktree path + branch.
entries.fp_expectation: low — fires only on terminal plan-Status flips (the command must contain 'Status' AND a terminal-state token DEFERRED/COMPLETED/ABANDONED/SUPERSEDED; prose edits like '## Status quo' pass), bulk plan mutations, git branch -D, and git worktree remove, and only when a live other-worktree checkout or a fresh (<2h) competing claim exists. Claims are REPO-scoped: each claim carries a repo identity field (origin push URL, else absolute git common dir) and the gate skips claims whose identity differs from the current repo's — a claim on repo A never blocks repo B; pre-schema claims lacking the field are skipped fail-open with a stderr note. Own-plan mutation (slug matches the current worktree's branch) never fires; worktree removal blocks only under a fresh other-session claim (never on mere existence); read-only bulk commands never fire. Known fail-open miss classes (hook-header KNOWN LIMITS): PowerShell-native mutations (Set-Content/Move-Item) and script-wrapped mutations (bash foo.sh) pass unexamined. Claim lifecycle: written at SessionStart, refreshed at each Stop, but NOT unclaimed at session end — claims persist up to 2h past a session's last turn, so stale-claim blocks in that window are expected (the structured waiver is the valve; unclaim wiring queued as CLAIM-LIFECYCLE-01 in docs/backlog.md). 19/19 self-test scenarios green including the golden bulk-defer block, a foreign-repo-claim ALLOW, an owned-plan prose-edit ALLOW, an unowned clean pass, and waiver-honored/stale/clause-less waiver paths.
entries.retirement_condition: if worktree-per-session claiming is enforced upstream (e.g. the launcher writes an authoritative per-branch lock the gate can trust exclusively), the git-worktree-list heuristic simplifies to a lock read; retire the heuristic half then. Also demotion-eligible via waiver-density per ADR 059 D7.
entries.honesty_rationale: Promotes parallel-dev-discipline Practice 8 (claim-before-touch) from self-applied Pattern to Mechanism — the lesson's root cause 4. World-fact check (git worktree list + fresh claims). Structured waiver hatch (fresh <1h, purpose clauses via lib/waiver-purpose-clause.sh, target-scoped, ledger-logged) per the ADR 059 D4 house pattern — present-moment friction, not consequence-deferred theater.

entries.honest_status: E.9a early-warning context watermark; wired PostToolUse at §E.W.

entries.golden_scenario: Downstream-product incident 2026-07-03 cross-repo write — a session whose project root is a different repo edits a neural-lace file (or vice versa) without the operator noticing until much later.
entries.fp_expectation: legitimate cross-repo harness sessions (main-checkout maintenance, orchestrator sessions operating from a linked worktree of the SAME repo) must not warn — both REQUIRED fixtures (main-checkout session, linked-worktree session) pass silently, 11/11 self-test scenarios.
entries.retirement_condition: zero fires for 30 days post-GA, or superseded by a stronger structural repo-isolation mechanism.
entries.honesty_rationale: WARN-only by design (specs-f §F.5 item 4) — never blocks; a legitimate cross-repo/orchestrator touch is common and blocking it would be strictly worse than the risk it flags. Points at nl-issue.sh as the canonical cross-project capture path.

entries.honest_status: replay retired to attic (fence retirement, D.4/D.5); pending-surfacer dispatched via surfacer-pack; reply-emit wired at UserPromptSubmit.

scripts/dispatch-provenance.sh — writes a per-dispatch provenance marker (cmd_write) recording which worktree/session a builder dispatch spawned into, read back by hooks/lib/progress-log-lib.sh's pl_classify_session (the Task 9 spawned-session guard) to distinguish a dispatched child session from a genuine new operator ask. Called by hooks/workstreams-emit.sh's task_started splice (the same --on-builder-dispatch call path that emits the task_started progress-log event, so the marker and the event share provenance for one dispatch). Not event-wired as its own settings.json entry — a one-line splice call-site inside an already-wired hook, mirroring the session-heartbeat convention. Inventory-only per the filed nl-issue note that manifest-check's disk-coverage check (b) only disk-scans hooks/*.sh top-level, never scripts/ — this entry exists for honesty, not enforcement.
invoked via pre-commit-gate.sh chain; not directly wired in settings.json.template
entries.honesty_rationale: Commit-time atomicity check (structural harness change needs its doc delta in the same commit) — resolvable by the session.

entries.honest_status: Dispatched via session-start-surfacer-pack.sh since D.5 (one SessionStart entry); E.1 digest replaces the pack.
scripts/end-manifest.sh (E.12) — session end-manifest writer+validator; invoked by the dispatcher when a manifest is present, not event-wired.

scripts/ensure-cockpit.sh — best-effort SessionStart ensure for the observability cockpit (workstreams-ui node server, port 7733); called by a one-line splice in session-start-digest.sh run_digest() (mirrors the session-heartbeat splice convention), not event-wired as its own settings.json entry. Guards: operator kill-switch (~/.claude/local/cockpit-disabled or ENSURE_COCKPIT_DISABLE=1), Windows-only, HARNESS_SELFTEST stub, machine-wide nl_repo_root resolution normalized to the MAIN checkout (never a worktree) with session-cwd fallback, nohup+disown non-blocking dispatch, tolerate-absent (always exit 0). Replaces the ConversationTreeUI-AutoStart logon scheduled task (retired at integration 2026-07-09).

entries.waiver_path: inline ENV_LOCAL_OVERWRITE_OK=1 command-prefix sentinel (documented equivalent; hook auto-backs-up before honoring it).

entries.honest_status: docs+skill unit only (skills/coordinate-estate/SKILL.md + doctrine/estate-coordination.md); no hook, no wiring; jit_triggers fire doctrine-jit.sh's paths-match on any edit whose file_path contains SCRATCHPAD.md (keywords reserved for v2 per schema, not yet matched).

entries.golden_scenario: 2026-07-14: three successive investigations of a live duplicate-appointment bug each reasoned from a plausible code path (missing per-contact uniqueness) to a shipped fix (#972), without ever observing the SPECIFIC incident's evidence (the two rows' created_at gap, booked_via, service_type, property). The fix shipped on an inferred-not-observed cause and introduced a multi-property regression the operator had to catch. This gate WARNS (does not yet block, see PROMOTION CONDITION in honest_status) on a fix(...)/fix: commit whose message lacks a PROVEN-tagged, citation-backed Root cause (evidenced) section or a verdict:PASS fix-root-cause review record covering a staged file, so the same mechanism-sufficient-fix shape gets a teaching banner even before the gate is calibrated enough to block it.
entries.fp_expectation: MEASURED, not estimated (harness-review REJECT remediation, 2026-07-16): `git log -400 --format=%s | grep -cE '^fix(\(|:)'` = 61/400 (~15%; reviewer's own -300 sample measured ~13%) of THIS repo's own commits match the trigger. A manual skim of that sample shows the DOMINANT class is harness-maintenance / review-remediation fixes ("fix(review): address harness-review findings", "fix(wave-o): ...", "fix(model-pin): resolve REJECT review findings") -- NOT incident-forensics-shaped bugs with an observable row/log to cite; for that class 'PROVEN root cause' in the incident-evidence sense often doesn't apply (the actual evidence IS a reviewer verdict, closer to path 2's record reference than path 1's citation). The original 'Low' estimate here was wrong -- it assumed the trigger population looked like the source incident, not like this repo's own commit history. Deliberate lighter path: fix-trivial: (a different prefix, never matches) for changes touching NO runtime/product code. Merge/rebase/cherry-pick replay contexts (MERGE_HEAD, CHERRY_PICK_HEAD, rebase-apply, rebase-merge, or a 'Merge branch' message) are fully skipped. See PROMOTION CONDITION below for what closes this gap before blocking is considered again.
entries.retirement_condition: Retire when harness-reviewer/code-reviewer natively enforce evidenced-root-cause at PR-review time with transcript-verifiable evidence (closing the same anti-fabrication residual review-before-deploy names), or when commit messages are structurally replaced by a form that cannot express free-text sections.
entries.honest_status: WARN-MODE-PENDING-CALIBRATION (harness-review REJECT remediation, 2026-07-16): evidence-before-fix-gate.sh ALWAYS exits 0 -- it never blocks a commit. On a triggering fix(/fix: commit lacking evidence it prints the full teaching banner (stderr + hookSpecificOutput.additionalContext) but the commit proceeds regardless. blocking:false reflects ACTUAL current behavior, not a designed end-state kept hidden behind a default -- unlike claude-md-hygiene's entry (blocking:true with a warn-mode default via env var), this entry is honest about the runtime being warn-only right now. PROMOTION CONDITION (tracked docs/backlog.md EVIDENCE-BEFORE-FIX-PROMOTION-01): promote to blocking:true only after a measured calibration period (method: the reviewer's own sweep, `git log -N --format=%s` bucketed into {incident-shaped, review/audit-remediation, refactor/typo, other}) shows the over-fire class (non-incident maintenance/review-remediation fixes, measured ~13-15% of this repo's fix(/fix: commits at the 2026-07-16 baseline) is EITHER separable by a trigger refinement OR acceptably rare once the parser-reach fixes below are reflected in a fresh measurement.
entries.honesty_rationale: STRUCTURE-NOT-TRUTH (named, not solved): the gate verifies a PROVEN-tagged line carries a citation-SHAPED token (file:line, backtick-quoted command/output, or a command:/output:/log: label) and, for the record-reference path, that a fix-root-cause record exists with verdict PASS and the required tag -- it CANNOT verify the citation is real, that the cited command was actually run, or that the record's quoted verdict is genuine (same anti-fabrication residual as review-before-deploy's Amendment C; the record path inherits its append-only audit trail as the compensating control, not a guarantee). A determined agent can fabricate a citation-shaped string or an inline section; this gate raises the cost, it does not make fabrication impossible. PARSER RESIDUAL (harness-review PROVEN, fixed for the common shapes, disclosed for the rest): message extraction now handles glued/spaced -m and --message (with or without =), multiple -m segments (concatenated), -F <file>, and a best-effort --amend-with-no-message proxy (git log -1 --format=%B, correct not stale) -- but a message built via complex nested shell interpolation the static command string doesn't literally contain, or an interactive --amend editor session, still silently produces no evidence check (lower-stakes under warn-mode than it was under blocking, since nothing is blocked either way, but a triggering commit in such a shape still gets no teaching banner).

entries.honest_status: Dispatched via session-start-surfacer-pack.sh since D.5 (one SessionStart entry); E.1 digest replaces the pack.

entries.golden_scenario: Agent-efficiency lesson 2026-07-13 Finding 3 — a session runs `find / -maxdepth 6 -iname <repo>` (a disk-wide scan) at ~65% of a core to locate the repo root instead of using Glob / git rev-parse.
entries.fp_expectation: scoped finds (`find .`, `find ./src`, `find adapters/`) must stay silent — 11/11 self-test scenarios; a false positive costs one stderr line, never a block.
entries.retirement_condition: zero fires for 30 days, or superseded by a stronger agent-doctrine mechanism that prevents disk-wide finds at the source.
entries.honesty_rationale: WARN-only by design (rec 6 of the efficiency lesson) — never blocks; scoped finds are common and legitimate; every code path exits 0. Nudges toward Glob/Grep/git rev-parse.
Schema-validity check on the session's own docs/findings.md edit — checked-box-without-evidence shape, unwaivable by ADR 059 D4 design.

entries.golden_scenario: A gate crosses the waiver-density.sh over-threshold line (>=3 waivers/week) and is a genuine auto-demotion candidate per ADR 059 D7.
entries.fp_expectation: gate-demotion.sh --self-test operates ONLY on manifest COPIES in a tempdir (16/16 scenarios green); --apply is the orchestrator-applied real-manifest-edit step, never auto-scheduled, so there is no session-time false-positive surface — a human/harness-reviewer reviews every real demotion.
entries.retirement_condition: superseded if ADR 059 D7's metric-driven demotion policy changes, or if waiver-density.sh itself is retired.
entries.honesty_rationale: Manually-invoked script (scripts/, not hooks/) in the same class as waiver-density.sh — invoked from the E.5 weekly KPI pass's --dry-run mode; --apply is a reviewed, orchestrator-applied action by design (ADR 059 D7), never a session-time gate.

entries.honest_status: scripts/gen-architecture-doc.sh (F.2) -- regenerates docs/harness-architecture.md from manifest.json; --check is the doctor drift predicate (tests/fixtures/wave-f/F.2/doctor-predicate.md); not event-wired (manual + doctor-invoked).

entries.honesty_rationale: Never blocks (every path exits 0) — it prepares the gh CLI environment (a pre-emptive `gh auth switch`) before the tool call proceeds, it never denies the tool call. Purely a side-effecting preparatory action, not a world-state assertion gate, so no waiver_path is offered (nothing to waive — there is no refusal to bypass).
entries.golden_scenario: A `gh pr merge`/`gh pr create`/`gh pr view`/`git push <remote>` command targets a repo owned by the OTHER gh account than the one currently active (e.g. active=alice-at-acme, target repo owned by alice-example, or vice versa). Without this hook the command 403s ("Repository not found") and the agent historically stopped and waited on the operator for a one-line `gh auth switch` fix. This hook resolves the target owner (via --repo flag, the git remote's URL, or the cwd repo's origin) and pre-emptively runs `gh auth switch -u <owner>` before the command executes, so the 403 never happens.
entries.fp_expectation: Near-zero false-positive risk since the hook never blocks or denies anything — the only 'cost' of a false-positive-shaped trigger is an unnecessary (but harmless, idempotent) `gh auth switch` call on a command that would have succeeded anyway (e.g. the owner heuristic misfires on a command that doesn't actually need a different account). A legitimate same-account session touching only one account's repos in a row triggers zero switches after the first (idempotent no-op path verified by self-test scenario S10). Read-only gh/git commands (gh pr list/gh repo list/gh api GET-shaped calls not in the write-subcommand list, plain `git fetch`/`git pull`) are explicitly excluded from the write-scope regex and never trigger a switch.
entries.retirement_condition: Retire or demote if: (a) gh CLI ships native multi-account auto-resolution making the manual switch step obsolete, (b) the operator consolidates to a single GitHub account (eliminating the dual-account problem this hook exists to solve), or (c) signal-ledger data over a full quarter shows zero 'warn' events with gate=gh-account-autoswitch (meaning the SessionStart directory-default switcher alone is sufficient and this PreToolUse layer is never actually needed).

entries.golden_scenario: 2026-07-15: the neural-lace masters diverged 14/10 after server-side PR merges landed on the work-org (pt) repo between reconciles (PR #100, 2026-07-13). PR #100's actual merge PATH (web-UI vs gh CLI) is UNKNOWN — server-side merges are indistinguishable in the API record — so THIS golden scenario validates server-side branch protection (the PRIMARY mechanism, operator-only), not this gate; the gate is honestly labeled defense-in-depth for the gh-CLI path only (decision 064 amendment A3).
entries.fp_expectation: Defined against the RESOLVED target, never the raw command string (A4): zero legitimate pt-repo `gh pr merge`/`gh api .../merge` calls are expected post-cutover — any such call is itself the defect the gate exists to catch, not a false positive. A genuinely ambiguous/unresolvable target (multiple or zero recognized remotes, no `gh repo set-default` state) fails loud and blocks rather than guessing; this is a distinct, teaching outcome, not a silent block of a personal-side merge — self-test proves personal-side and gh-resolved-personal targets always ALLOW (23/23, including the explicit FP-fear scenario and the 2026-07-16 harness-review fixup scenarios: flat-shape `.command` payload, multi-space `gh  pr  merge`, positional PR-URL/`OWNER/REPO#N`, and gh-api `{owner}/{repo}` template placeholders).
entries.retirement_condition: Retire when the pt repo is archived, OR the operator enables GitHub server-side branch protection on pt/master (at which point this gate is a redundant teaching surface for the gh-CLI path and may be retired or kept as UX).
entries.honest_status: gh-merge-canonical-gate.sh (PreToolUse Bash) BLOCKS `gh pr merge` / `gh api .../pulls/N/merge` when the RESOLVED target repo equals the `pt` remote's repo (read from `git remote get-url pt` at runtime, never hardcoded). DEFENSE-IN-DEPTH ONLY (decision 064 amendments A1/A2) — the PRIMARY structural mechanism is server-side branch protection on pt/master (operator-only, not agent-executable, surfaced as a standing NEEDS-YOU item until enabled). Scopes itself by the remote NAME `pt` (a neural-lace-specific convention); on any other repo across the estate that names its mirror remote something else, this gate is a silent no-op for that repo. Target resolution (post 2026-07-16 harness-review fixup) reads `.tool_input.command` with a `.command` flat-shape fallback, tolerates irregular whitespace in `gh pr merge`, and parses positional PR-URL / `OWNER/REPO#N` targets ahead of default-repo resolution; a gh-api `{owner}/{repo}` template placeholder is treated as not-explicit (falls through) rather than as a literal never-matching owner/repo. HONEST RESIDUAL — invocation shapes still uncovered by the parser (fall through to default-repo resolution or the loud ambiguous-block, never misread as a false ALLOW/BLOCK): a runtime-interpolated value (`gh pr merge $N --repo "$REPO_VAR"` — invisible to a static-string check); a bundled short flag with no space (`-Rowner/repo`); a `gh pr merge` invoked via a shell alias/function whose name doesn't literally contain adjacent `gh`/`pr`/`merge` tokens. Also not covered by this gate at all (A1): GitHub web-UI merges, a machine that hasn't yet synced this hook (deploy-lag window), un-harnessed/external machines, CI/GitHub Actions, scheduled/cloud agents (Decision 011 — no PreToolUse), direct `git push pt master`.
entries.waiver_path: none — a pt-repo merge is the exact defect this gate exists to catch, not to waive. Fails OPEN only on internal error (no jq / malformed input / no `pt` remote configured in this checkout), never on a genuine resolved pt-repo target.

entries.waiver_path: ~/.claude/state/local-edit-<slug>-<ISO8601>.txt, fresh <30min, written only by the operator-invoked /grant-local-edit skill.

entries.honest_status: scripts/master-drift-autocorrect.sh — FF-only remote-master drift corrector (docs/plans/master-drift-autocorrection-2026-07.md). Not event-wired itself: dispatched BACKGROUNDED by hooks/session-start-git-freshness.sh (the git-freshness entry's hook) on remote-vs-remote master SHA inequality or a non-quiet ~/.claude/state/master-drift/<repo>.status, and directly invocable by hand. All mutating git ops run in the F.6 dedicated sync clone (~/.claude/sync-clone/<repo>); true divergence is surfaced (one digest line), never auto-merged; kill switch MASTER_DRIFT_AUTOCORRECT=0. Doctor predicate: check_master_drift_autocorrect (quick: script + --self-test entrypoint + hook wiring; --full additionally runs the script's --self-test). Runbook: docs/runbooks/master-drift-autocorrect.md.
entries.golden_scenario: The repo's two masters (origin = personal remote, mirror = work-org remote) push-race-diverged SIX times on 2026-07-12 alone: local sessions dual-push both masters, but a GitHub SERVER-SIDE PR merge lands on exactly one of them, stranding the other until a human discovers the drift mid-push days later (the failure that cost the 2026-07-11 reconciliation session). This mechanism fast-forwards the strictly-behind master within one session start (FF-behind shape) and surfaces true divergence as exactly one [master-drift] DIVERGED digest line pointing at the runbook (surface-only shape) — never an auto-merge.
entries.fp_expectation: Near-zero: the corrector never blocks anything (exit 0 in every path, backgrounded off the session-start blocking path) and mutates only when `git merge-base --is-ancestor` proves one master strictly behind the other; a converged pair is a pure read (CONVERGED, zero digest lines). The riskiest legitimate shape — a human dual-push racing the check-to-push window — degrades to the server's non-FF rejection recorded as PUSH-REJECTED (no retry loop, next session re-evaluates), never to a forced or wrong push. A legitimately diverged pair is surfaced for a reviewed merge, never touched.
entries.retirement_condition: Retire if the dual-remote topology is consolidated to a single remote (no second master to drift), if server-side merges stop landing on exactly one remote (e.g. a platform-native mirror is adopted, reversing the 5bf55c7 operator revert), or if a full quarter of ~/.claude/state/master-drift/ status history shows only CONVERGED (the drift class has gone extinct and detection alone suffices).
entries.honesty_rationale: Never blocks and never refuses an action — a background writer: it either fast-forwards the strictly-behind master (plain push, server rejection as backstop) or records a one-line status for the digest to surface. There is no gate verdict to waive; the operator-facing off-switch is the MASTER_DRIFT_AUTOCORRECT=0 kill switch honored by both the dispatching hook and the script itself.

entries.honest_status: hooks/lib/merge-scan-lib.sh — ms_emit_merged_for_commit (per-commit `merged` progress-log event, natural-keyed on commit SHA) + ms_scan_repo_for_merges (git-log backfill lane reconciling any missed `merged` events, Task 5b/12). Called by adapters/claude-code/git-hooks/post-commit's post-commit hook body (local-only, best-effort, never blocks the commit) and by the workstreams-ui background auditor's git-log comparison pass. Lives under hooks/lib/ — a subdirectory manifest-check's disk-coverage check (b) never scans (only top-level hooks/*.sh is scanned), so it carries no hooks[] entry of its own; this entry is inventory-only, included for honesty per the filed nl-issue note on manifest-check's disk-scope.

entries.golden_scenario: 2026-07-14: a Fable (premium-tier) main-loop spawned un-pinned subagents that silently INHERITED Fable, draining ~1.7M tokens before the budget cap. The gate blocks any Task/Agent spawn that passes no explicit model AND whose agent type is unpinned/unknown, forcing an explicit model choice so silent premium-tier inheritance cannot recur.
entries.fp_expectation: Low. All 24 repo agents are pinned (enforced by the model-pins doctor check). Known FP surfaces are handled: a display-name subagent_type (e.g. 'Domain Expert Tester') is resolved via name: frontmatter, not filename only; the 'fork' subagent_type is exempted (it always inherits the parent model by design and cannot be pinned or model-overridden). Residual: a brand-new UNPINNED custom agent type spawned with NO model is blocked — that is the intended enforcement, not a false positive, and the remedy (pass model: on the spawn) is always achievable (verified valid Agent-tool param, used in-session 2026-07-14).
entries.retirement_condition: Retire when Claude Code natively requires an explicit model on every subagent spawn (making silent inheritance impossible at the tool layer), OR when the main-loop model is guaranteed never to be a premium/budget-sensitive tier.
entries.honest_status: model-pin-gate.sh (PreToolUse Task|Agent) BLOCKS a subagent spawn with no explicit model whose agent type is unpinned/unknown — forcing explicit model selection so a spawn never SILENTLY INHERITS the main-loop model (operator directive 2026-07-14; silent Fable inheritance drained ~1.7M tokens). Source of truth config/model-policy.json; all 24 agents/*.md pinned. HONEST RESIDUAL (NOT gate-able, doctrine/model-selection.md): Workflow-inline agent(), spawn_task, cron/remote expose no model field a PreToolUse hook can inspect — convention + review only. A harness-doctor check (model-pins) enforces that every agents/*.md stays pinned.
entries.waiver_path: none — an unpinned spawn is a bug to fix (pass model: or pin the agent), not to waive. Fails OPEN only on internal error (no jq / malformed input), never on a genuine missing-model.

entries.honest_status: scripts/needs-you.sh — maintains NEEDS-YOU.md (E.6); called by decision-log flow + digest, not event-wired. Cold-reader lint (constitution §3 amendment 53d3bee, operator directive 2026-07-07): `add --section decision` scores every new entry's --text against three zero-session-context checks (background/context, a concrete artifact anchor, per-option outcome text — see _ny_lint_decision_text) and stores the result as a `lint_warnings` array on the item. TWO-PATH contract (T4/A1 cold-reader block-promotion, cockpit-roadmap-redesign 2026-07-20): an INTERACTIVE `add --section decision` whose --text fails the lint BLOCKS (exit 1, nothing written, teaching message printed) so the session retries with context; a `--mechanical` caller (stop-verdict-dispatcher, session-resumer, session-honesty-gate PAUSING) STORES-AND-QUARANTINES instead (exit 0, `lint_warnings` stamped, NEVER rejected — the ledger-never-rejects contract is preserved so a waiting item never lands nowhere). Golden scenario: the 2026-07-18 bare-token sign-off incident; retirement: demote to warn if weekly triage shows FP blocks exceeding true catches. Self-tested (T22-T25 + interactive-block + mechanical-quarantine).

scripts/nl.sh (C5 dispatcher) + hooks/lib/observability-derive.sh (C4 pure-read derivation lib: od_sessions/od_needs_me/od_shipped_since/od_harness_health/od_costs/od_backlog_health/od_why) — the six-question observability CLI (specs-o §O.3). Read-only, zero state writes; not event-wired (invoked on demand by the operator or by the future §O.4 cockpit server shelling out to `nl <sub> --json`).

entries.golden_scenario: Operator misses a new NEEDS-YOU entry, a session stalling out unattended for hours, or a doctor RED regression because nothing interrupts them outside the terminal — the 2026-07-04 design sketch's push surface exists to close exactly this gap for the three classes the operator actually wants an interrupt for.
entries.fp_expectation: Near-zero: push only fires for a genuinely NEW item-id per class (deduped forever via sent.jsonl) and only once a real ntfy topic is configured; an unknown --class is rejected before any network attempt (self-tested negative) so no other event type can ever reach this channel. Legitimate no-push cases (topic not yet configured, no new items, doctor staying green) are silent — never a false push.
entries.retirement_condition: Retire or fold into a broader alerting mechanism if the operator reports push volume becoming noise (more than a few pushes/week outside genuine incidents), or if the O.4 cockpit's own visibility make phone push redundant for all three classes per the O.7 retro's operator-trust check.
entries.honesty_rationale: Not a gate — a best-effort external notification writer. send/scan never block or fail any caller (needs-you.sh's call site is fire-and-forget; the scheduled scan tick's own exit code is not consumed by anything blocking).

ORCHESTRATOR RECONCILIATION (batch 2): O.3's fragment proposed the same jit_triggers.paths (scripts/nl.sh, hooks/lib/observability-derive.sh) as the sibling `nl-cli` entry above; doctrine-jit.sh's _compute_injection returns on the FIRST array-order match (see hooks/doctrine-jit.sh ~line 283), so with identical paths on both entries `nl-cli` (earlier in array order) would always win and this entry's own trigger would never fire — a dead, shadowed config. Resolved toward path-disjoint triggers: `nl-cli` (the mechanism entry) keeps the paths; this entry (the doctrine entry, same doctrine_file) keeps only its keywords, which the schema documents as reserved/not yet mechanically matched in v1 but preserved as documentation-of-intent.

entries.honest_status: hooks/lib/progress-log-lib.sh (pl_emit/pl_path_for/pl_classify_session — the shared writer lib: natural-key dedup, sandbox-only-writes, per-ask JSONL under ~/.claude/state/progress-logs) + scripts/progress-log.sh (the stable `emit` CLI wrapper every splice below shells out to; no splice sources the lib directly) — the ask-rooted-workstreams-p1 progress-log writer family (Tasks 1-6, 9). Every emission site is a one-line splice inside an ALREADY-wired hook or script, never its own settings.json entry, mirroring the session-heartbeat convention. Emitting splices, named verbatim: (1) hooks/plan-lifecycle.sh's emit_task_done_progress_log_events (task_done, on task-verifier checkbox flip) and emit_plan_amended_progress_log_events (plan_amended, on newly-introduced task lines / scope-section edits); (2) hooks/workstreams-emit.sh's task_started splice (best-effort on --on-builder-dispatch) firing alongside scripts/dispatch-provenance.sh's marker write (cmd_write) for the same dispatch; (3) scripts/needs-you.sh's Task-4 splice: waiting_on_operator emission plus a docs/operator-todo.md auto-pointer append, each independently best-effort-wrapped; (4) hooks/lib/merge-scan-lib.sh's ms_emit_merged_for_commit (merged event) called from adapters/claude-code/git-hooks/post-commit's post-commit hook body, backfilled by the auditor's ms_scan_repo_for_merges git-log scan (Task 5b/12); (5) scripts/close-plan.sh's emit_plan_completed_progress_log_event (plan_completed, the sixth/exit lane), reached via both the wired plan-auto-closure.sh PostToolUse hook and manual `close-plan.sh close` runs; (6) hooks/workstreams-read.sh's first-prompt ask_registered capture splice (calls scripts/ask-registry.sh register, guarded against spawned/builder/sub-agent sessions via pl_classify_session) and hooks/session-start-digest.sh's session_attached splice beside the existing heartbeat splice (calls scripts/ask-registry.sh attach-session on resume/spawn). See the sibling `ask-registry`, `dispatch-provenance`, and `merge-scan` manifest entries for those three scripts' own honest_status detail.

PATTERN — self-applied. The law (reap what you initiate, on every exit path) is doctrine, NOT yet a Mechanism: the code-reviewer/architecture-reviewer lens that would flag timeout-without-kill and spawn-without-tree-kill is proposed in the -full companion but not wired. Golden case: auditor.js runCli timeout-without-kill leaked 781 bash.exe / 3 reboots (fixed 2026-07-14).
Dispatched via session-start-surfacer-pack.sh since D.5 (one SessionStart entry); E.1 digest replaces the pack.

Not represented in hooks[] (the schema restricts hooks[] to bare *.sh basenames directly under adapters/claude-code/hooks/, which excludes both scripts/ and hooks/lib/). The actual mechanism: scripts/write-review-record.sh (writer, self-test) + hooks/lib/review-record-gate-lib.sh (shared surface/coverage lib, self-test), consumed by a hard-block splice in adapters/claude-code/install.sh (manual, operator-present) and a fail-open skip+warn splice in adapters/claude-code/hooks/session-start-auto-install.sh (SessionStart -- already separately cataloged under the session-start-auto-install entry, whose own hooks[] is unaffected by this addition).
entries.golden_scenario: 2026-07-14 (two proven misses, model-enforcement workstream): a prior session live-synced a buggy model-pin-gate.sh to ~/.claude with zero harness-reviewer PASS; the same session later `install.sh`-deployed a fix before its re-review returned. The gate blocks (install.sh) or skips+warns (auto-install) any covered changed file lacking a PASS harness-change-review record, so neither miss can recur silently. NOTE: 937e8cb (a merge that silently dropped 5 pt-side files) is explicitly OUT of this gate's coverage per Amendment B -- it is a content-ABSENCE defect, not a content-presence one; the merge-time dropped-side sweep (docs/runbooks/master-reconcile-and-estate-cleanup.md step 6) is the mechanism for that class.
entries.fp_expectation: Low-moderate on install.sh (hard block, operator-present -- a false block is immediately visible and the remedy is always 'get a PASS review or wait'). A fresh/long-stale machine cannot be bricked: the cutover grandfather-manifest.json covers every trigger-surface file's pre-cutover blob_sha, so only genuinely NEW content ever requires a record. Known deliberate over-inclusion: an agents/*.md wording-only edit still requires a PASS (no cheap way to distinguish cosmetic from behavioral agent-prompt edits). session-start-auto-install.sh's skip+warn path has effectively ZERO fp COST (it never blocks a session; it only leaves one file stale one sync cycle later than usual). Enforced-set parity (2026-07-16 REFORMULATE fixup): both carriers walk hooks/**/*.sh, scripts/**/*.sh, and rules/** RECURSIVELY (not a flat top-level glob), so the hard-block path can no longer be weaker than the fail-open path (a flat scripts/*.sh glob previously missed scripts/lib/*.sh); settings.json.template is gated at BOTH its call sites (--replace-settings and the normal missing-settings.json copy). Named residual: config/** is part of the trigger surface but is deployed by NEITHER carrier today (config/model-policy.json reaches no live mirror) -- not a gate hole, a pre-existing deployment gap tracked separately.
entries.retirement_condition: Retire the install.sh hard-block half when install.sh itself is retired in favor of a single continuously-reconciling sync path; retire entirely if a real anti-fabrication anchor (see honesty_rationale) plus a native platform review-gate make this record-based check redundant.
entries.honesty_rationale: Anti-fabrication residual named, not solved (Amendment C): the gate checks review-record EXISTENCE + content-match only, and CANNOT verify the reviewer's quoted verdict is genuine -- zero SubagentStop/TaskCompleted capture hooks exist in this harness to retrieve a real transcript. This record is an audit + honesty anchor, not a deploy-path anti-fabrication control; see docs/design-notes/review-record-primitive.md's Writer section and the REVIEW-RECORD-ANTI-FABRICATION-ANCHOR-01 backlog follow-up.

E.11 batched Stop verdict; invokes work-integrity/session-honesty/bug-persistence in --report mode, aggregates one verdict; replaces their 3 blocking Stop entries at §E.W (Stop 6->4). pin-f: delegates to the gates that validate purpose clauses. Cold-reader-lint WARN (constitution §3 amendment 53d3bee, operator directive 2026-07-07), following FUNCTIONAL-LINK's own precedent immediately above it in this same file: scans the final assistant message for a §3-format "Decision needed" block and, if it is missing an artifact anchor or per-option outcome text, emits ONE ledger_emit warn + a stderr notice. WARN-only — never contributes to the block/gap verdict above, never participates in cycle-counting/DONE-refusal, never touches stdout. Self-tested (scenarios renumbered 20-23 at batch-integration to avoid colliding with the FIX-2a/FIX-2b automation-ceiling scenarios 18-19 already on master; 3 of 4 pass). KNOWN BUG (found during batch integration, confirmed pre-existing on the source branch in isolation, not introduced by the merge): `_svd_message_has_decision_block`'s heuristic does a naive case-insensitive substring match for `decision needed`, so prose that negates it (e.g. "no decision needed here") still matches and false-positive-warns as a decision block missing an anchor — 1 self-test scenario (ordinary-prose-not-scanned) fails on this. Low severity (WARN-only, never blocks) but real; follow-up filed to require the heuristic to exclude a preceding negation token.
entries.honesty_rationale: Aggregator; delegates to bug-persistence/work-integrity/session-honesty's own waiver postures (already stated in its honest_status).

entries.golden_scenario: A dispatched builder worktree crashes, is SIGKILLed/OOM-killed, or survives a machine reboot mid-turn, leaving uncommitted edits and/or a committed-but-never-cherry-picked branch behind while its liveness signal goes stale and the owning process is dead (or the machine simply never came back up) — INCLUDING a dispatched plan-phase-builder subagent (isolation:worktree, worktree named `agent-<id>`), which writes NO heartbeat of its own (only a top-level session's heartbeat writer runs), so the ORIGINAL heartbeat-only design would classify an ACTIVELY-RUNNING builder's dirty worktree as stranded on every parallel-build day (the harness-review REFORMULATE finding this entry's current fp_expectation documents the fix for) — AND, critically (harness-review ROUND-2 finding, 2026-07-17), the dispatch/isolation mechanism `git worktree lock`s an agent's worktree for the FULL duration of its dispatch, unlocking only on clean completion, so a crashed/SIGKILLed/OOM-killed agent's worktree stays `locked` FOREVER (proven on the reviewing machine: dead-pid locked worktrees persisting after the owning process is confirmed dead via `tasklist`, since `kill -0` is unreliable on that MSYS setup) — a shape the round-1 transcript-mtime fix still MISSED, because the sweep's pre-existing `*,locked,*` structural-skip returned before the liveness split ever ran for ANY locked worktree (agent or not), making the crash class this very golden_scenario field claimed to catch UNREACHABLE for the locked case specifically until this round's fix. The shared detector (scripts/worktree-hygiene-sweep.sh --stranded[--porcelain]) joins that worktree's dirty/unintegrated state against session-heartbeat-lib.sh's liveness classification (named/non-agent worktrees, locked or not — unchanged) or the agent's own transcript-mtime (agent-<id> worktrees, LOCKED OR NOT as of round 2) and surfaces it on the very next SessionStart digest (session-start-digest.sh's feed_stranded_work) and the next harness-doctor.sh --quick (check_orphaned_worktree_work), naming the worktree path, branch, dirty/unintegrated counts, and the exact salvage command (including the `git worktree unlock` step a locked row needs, since `git worktree remove` refuses on a locked worktree and a single --force is not enough) — rather than the work silently rotting until a manual `git worktree list` audit.
entries.fp_expectation: Low. REFORMULATED (2026-07-1x, docs/harness-improvements/orphaned-worktree-guard.md) after a harness-reviewer REFORMULATE verdict on the original heartbeat-only join: a dispatched plan-phase-builder subagent (isolation:worktree) writes NO heartbeat of its own, so the original design would classify an ACTIVELY-RUNNING builder's dirty `agent-<id>` worktree as stranded on every parallel-build day — a cry-wolf false positive the review correctly blocked on. Fix: for a worktree whose basename matches `agent-*` (the harness's own dispatch-time naming), `_live_owner` treats that agent's OWN transcript file mtime (`<session>/subagents/agent-<id>.jsonl`, appended to continuously while the agent works — verified against this session's own transcript, not assumed) as the PRIMARY liveness signal, checked before the heartbeat/claim join and requiring no dispatch-path change. ROUND 2 (harness-review, 2026-07-17): the round-1 fix above was itself found to be UNREACHABLE for a locked agent worktree — `classify_worktree`'s pre-existing `*,locked,*` structural-skip returned BEFORE the liveness split (and therefore before the transcript-mtime check) ever ran, for ANY locked worktree. Proven on the reviewing machine: the dispatch/isolation mechanism locks an agent's worktree for the full dispatch duration and only unlocks on clean completion, so THREE real dead-pid locked worktrees existed on that machine at review time, none of them ever reaching this detector's liveness logic at all. Fixed: `classify_worktree` no longer lets `locked` preempt an `agent-*`-named worktree specifically — it falls through to the normal dirty/unique computation and the liveness split, exactly as an unlocked agent worktree would, with `_live_owner` now taking a third `is_locked` argument so a stale-transcript-and-locked verdict reports the distinct label `agent-crashed-locked` (vs. plain `agent-transcript-stale` for stale-but-unlocked) — informative for salvage, since `git worktree remove` refuses on a locked worktree (a single `--force` is NOT enough; verified: `git worktree unlock <path>` first, or `git worktree remove -f -f <path>` [force TWICE], are the only ways through). A LOCKED but FRESH-transcript agent worktree is UNCHANGED-live: lock status carries no liveness information on its own (a live, actively-working agent is ALSO locked for the same reason a crashed one is) — only transcript freshness decides. Non-agent locked worktrees are UNCHANGED (still structural-skip; a manually-locked worktree, e.g. on removable media, is presumed intentionally persistent, unlike a dispatch-locked agent worktree). Also fixed in this round: `_build_agent_tx_cache`'s `find -maxdepth 6` was measured to include the Workflow-dispatch transcript variant (`.../subagents/workflows/wf_<id>/agent-<id>.jsonl`) at EXACTLY depth 6 — zero slack, the same class of silent-miss risk this whole reformulation exists to close — bumped to `-maxdepth 7`. The six mandated non-fires are now structurally guaranteed, not heuristic: (i) a still-live AGENT worktree (locked or not, as of round 2) is excluded via a transcript mtime within AGENT_TX_FRESH_MIN (default 30, same as OBS_STALE_MIN) minutes of now; (ii) a still-live NAMED (non-agent) session's worktree is excluded via the heartbeat/transcript-mtime liveness join (hb_classify live/throttled, including the mid-turn stale-heartbeat-but-fresh-transcript override, plus a CONTINUING-marker grace window for a builder standing by awaiting a scheduled wake) and the invoking session's own SELF-worktree exclusion; (iii) an already-integrated branch is excluded via git-cherry patch-equivalence PLUS merge-base --is-ancestor across all four candidate integration refs (origin/master, origin/main, master, main), so a local merge/cherry-pick that origin merely lags is never false-flagged; (iv) the orchestrator's own primary checkout is excluded twice over (the sweep's unconditional primary-skip plus the SELF check); (v) an agent-transcript cache lookup can only ADD ownership for `agent-*`-named worktrees specifically (anchored exact-basename match) — it is structurally inert for every non-agent worktree, so it can never suppress or alter the pre-existing heartbeat-based verdict for a named session; (vi) a LOCKED agent worktree that is genuinely still live is never false-flagged either — `is_locked` only ever narrows the VERDICT LABEL on an already-orphaned classification (agent-transcript-stale vs. agent-crashed-locked), it never feeds into the fresh/stale decision itself, so a live-but-locked agent takes the exact same early `agent-transcript-fresh` return path as a live-and-unlocked one. RESIDUAL (named, not eliminated — accepted for a WARN-only, never-auto-pruning surfacer): an agent that goes silent for longer than AGENT_TX_FRESH_MIN while genuinely still working (one very long reasoning/tool call with no intermediate transcript flush) could false-fire past the window; widening the window would materially delay real-orphan detection for the far more common crash/stall cases this entry exists to catch, so the tradeoff is kept at parity with the session-level OBS_STALE_MIN default rather than widened further. Also still bounded to the mode-(b) standing-by window past its grace and MSYS pid-liveness unreliability (mitigated by preferring transcript-mtime, which is platform-independent, over a pid check) — all advisory-only and non-destructive (nothing is ever auto-pruned; a locked worktree is additionally NEVER SAFE-PRUNE regardless of this fix, both because `git worktree remove` itself refuses on locked and because `classify_worktree` explicitly excludes `is_locked` from the SAFE-PRUNE promotion). worktree-hygiene-sweep.sh's own --self-test covers every excluded case explicitly (round-1: 39/39 — the original 34 live/throttled/CONTINUING-grace/fresh-claim/already-merged/self exclusions plus 5 REFORMULATION assertions for fresh/stale/unaffected-non-agent; round 2 adds 4 more proving (d) a LOCKED agent worktree with a stale transcript and content is ORPHANED with the agent-crashed-locked verdict and salvage text naming the unlock step [3 assertions], and (e) a LOCKED agent worktree with a FRESH transcript stays LIVE-OWNED, unaffected by the lock [1 assertion] — verified by an actual run: 43/43 total, 0 failed); the doctor adds its own agent-worktree fixture (oww-agent-live-green: dirty + zero heartbeat coverage + fresh transcript -> no WARN) on top of its pre-existing dirty/clean/live-owned fixtures (WARN present for a dirty orphan, absent for clean/merged/live-owned); both the doctor and the digest delegate entirely to this same detector, so they inherit both rounds' fixes with no changes of their own beyond the doctor's own fixture additions.
entries.retirement_condition: Once spawn-worktree writes an authoritative per-worktree lease and a SessionEnd unclaim lands, BOTH the heartbeat/claim join (named worktrees) AND the agent-transcript-mtime join (agent-<id> worktrees) simplify to a lease read — retire both heuristics then (the detector's dirty/unintegrated math is unaffected either way; this is the design's own P2 path, doubly motivated now that it would also retire the transcript-mtime heuristic's residual false-fire window, not just the heartbeat one). Also demotion-eligible via waiver-density per ADR 059 D7 if false positives prove costlier in practice than this analysis predicts.
entries.honesty_rationale: WARN-only in the doctor (never RED — check_orphaned_worktree_work never contributes to RED_COUNT, so it can never fail a --quick run on its own) and purely advisory in the SessionStart digest (one folded [stranded-work] line, silent when nothing is stranded — never a block). Nothing is ever auto-pruned: worktree-hygiene-sweep.sh's own --prune path still refuses every HOLDS-CONTENT worktree (including the ORPHANED-HOLDS-CONTENT split this entry covers) regardless of this mechanism, so a false positive costs one glance, never a lost worktree or a blocked session — including the new agent-transcript-stale/agent-crashed-locked false-fire residuals named in fp_expectation, which cost the same one glance and nothing more. The round-2 locked-worktree fix additionally can never widen the blast radius of a false positive into an actual removal: a locked worktree is doubly protected from --prune (excluded from SAFE-PRUNE classification by classify_worktree's own is_locked guard, AND git worktree remove itself refuses on a locked worktree at the git level, independent of anything this script decides) — surfacing a locked agent worktree as stranded now, where it was silently invisible before, only ever adds visibility, never a new deletion path.
